\end{tabular}
\begin{tablenotes}[flushleft]
\footnotesize
\item Portfolios are formed in June of each year by independent double-sorts on size (market equity, ME) into 10 deciles and pre-ranking beta into 10 deciles within each size group.
\item Size decile 1 (S1) is smallest; beta decile 1 (B1) is lowest beta.
\item Post-ranking betas are estimated using 24–60 monthly returns after portfolio formation.
\item Market equity is measured in RMB millions.
\end{tablenotes}
\end{threeparttable}
\end{table}

\item Panel A: decile 1 has the smallest market equity (ME).

\end{tablenotes}

\begin{table}[htbp]
\centering
\caption{Table III: Fama--MacBeth cross-sectional regressions --- average slopes from monthly regressions of stock returns on beta, size, and book-to-market}
\label{tab:fama_macbeth}
\small
\begin{threeparttable}
\begin{tabular}{lccccccc}
\toprule
 & (1) & (2) & (3) & (4) & (5) & (6) & (7) \\
\midrule
Intercept & 0.0179$^{***}$ & 0.0913$^{***}$ & -0.0069 & 0.1067$^{***}$ & 0.0029 & 0.0774$^{***}$ & 0.0918$^{**}$ \\
{\footnotesize} & {\footnotesize (2.787)} & {\footnotesize (3.890)} & {\footnotesize (-0.641)} & {\footnotesize (3.485)} & {\footnotesize (0.283)} & {\footnotesize (2.641)} & {\footnotesize (2.535)} \\
\addlinespace[0.3em]
$\beta$ & -0.0058 &  &  & -0.0044 & -0.0054 &  & -0.0040 \\
{\footnotesize} & {\footnotesize (-1.527)} & {\footnotesize } & {\footnotesize } & {\footnotesize (-1.532)} & {\footnotesize (-1.417)} & {\footnotesize } & {\footnotesize (-1.443)} \\
\addlinespace[0.3em]
$\ln(ME)$ &  & -0.0053$^{***}$ &  & -0.0062$^{***}$ &  & -0.0055$^{***}$ & -0.0060$^{***}$ \\
{\footnotesize} & {\footnotesize } & {\footnotesize (-3.774)} & {\footnotesize } & {\footnotesize (-3.075)} & {\footnotesize } & {\footnotesize (-3.493)} & {\footnotesize (-2.928)} \\
\addlinespace[0.3em]
$\ln(BE/ME)$ &  &  & 0.0035$^{**}$ &  & 0.0024 & 0.0030$^{*}$ & 0.0020 \\
{\footnotesize} & {\footnotesize } & {\footnotesize } & {\footnotesize (2.098)} & {\footnotesize } & {\footnotesize (1.587)} & {\footnotesize (1.847)} & {\footnotesize (1.249)} \\
\addlinespace[0.3em]
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\begin{tablenotes}[flushleft]
\footnotesize
\item Each column reports time-series averages of monthly Fama--MacBeth (1973) cross-sectional regression slopes.
\item Newey--West (1987) $t$-statistics with 12 lags are in parentheses.
\item $^{*}p<0.10$, $^{**}p<0.05$, $^{***}p<0.01$.
\item Returns are measured as raw monthly returns. $\ln(ME)$ is log market equity at June-end; $\ln(BE/ME)$ is log book-to-market measured in December of the prior year.
\end{tablenotes}
\end{threeparttable}
\end{table}

\small

\begin{tablenotes}[flushleft]
\footnotesize
\item Portfolios are formed in June of each year by sorting all stocks into 10 equal groups on book-to-market equity (BE/ME).
\item Decile 1 (M1) has the lowest BE/ME; decile 10 has the highest.
\item $\ln(ME)$ and $\ln(BE/ME)$ are portfolio averages of firm-level June characteristics used in monthly memberships.
\end{tablenotes}
\end{threeparttable}
\end{table}
